description: Configure the Jitsi Meet instance as a branded, authenticated conference platform called 'SecureConf' and verify the complete meeting flow works end-to-end. The platform must enforce internal authentication: only registered users can host meetings, while guests can still join. Edit /home/ga/jitsi/.env to set ENABLE_AUTH=1, AUTH_TYPE=internal, and ENABLE_GUESTS=1. Register an admin account (username: admin, password: SecureConf2024!) on the meet.jitsi XMPP domain. Restart all Jitsi services so changes take effect. Brand the interface by creating /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-interface_config.js: set the app name to 'SecureConf', restrict the toolbar to exactly microphone, camera, chat, tileview, hangup, and participants-pane, and disable mobile app promotion. Verify end-to-end: navigate Firefox to http://localhost:8080/VerificationRoom, authenticate as admin, join the meeting as moderator, and enable the lobby. Then open Epiphany to the same room URL so a guest attempts to join -- the guest should be held in the lobby. Admit the guest from the moderator view in Firefox. Save a deployment report to /home/ga/secure_conference_report.txt.

natural_language.prompt:
Configure the Jitsi Meet instance as a branded, authenticated conference platform called "SecureConf" and verify the complete meeting flow works end-to-end.

The platform must enforce internal authentication: only registered users can host meetings, while guests can still join. Edit /home/ga/jitsi/.env to set ENABLE_AUTH=1, AUTH_TYPE=internal, and ENABLE_GUESTS=1. Register an admin account (username: admin, password: SecureConf2024!) on the meet.jitsi XMPP domain. Restart all Jitsi services so changes take effect.

Brand the interface by creating /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-interface_config.js: set the app name to "SecureConf", restrict the toolbar to exactly microphone, camera, chat, tileview, hangup, and participants-pane, and disable mobile app promotion.

Verify end-to-end: navigate Firefox to http://localhost:8080/VerificationRoom, authenticate as admin, join the meeting as moderator, and enable the lobby. Then open Epiphany to the same room URL so a guest attempts to join -- the guest should be held in the lobby. Admit the guest from the moderator view in Firefox.

Save a deployment report to /home/ga/secure_conference_report.txt.
